summary: Bryan Cantrill reports from JavaOne 2005 where DTrace was featured in the morning keynote, with Java support announced to audience applause. Adam Leventhal gave a live demo using real DTrace scripts rather than pre-canned presentation software, and the scripts were published so anyone could replicate the demo. The post also promotes an OpenSolaris BOF session and highlights Solaris's growing appeal to Java developers following IBM's announcement of Solaris support.
key_points: DTrace Java support announced at JavaOne keynote to enthusiastic response
Live demo used actual DTrace rather than canned demo software
Demo scripts were open-sourced and blogged for public use
IBM announced Solaris support the same day
OpenSolaris BOF session held to showcase Solaris technologies to Java developers
money_quotes: The most remarkable aspect of which is that it was actually DTrace and not the canned, gussied-up gimcrack that normally passes for a keynote demo.
In fact, this demo was so tangibly real, Adam has blogged the scripts from the demo. With these scripts, you can now give Adam's keynote in the comfort of your own home!
Solaris has never been more attractive to the Java developer.
key_insight: Authentic technical demos that share their actual code are more valuable and credible than polished but fake keynote presentations.
